sections.title: Execution, names, objects, and identity
sections.why: Build the object-and-binding mental model that explains assignment, aliasing, identity, and mutation.
sections.lab: Trace bindings and mutations with id(), copy.copy(), and copy.deepcopy(); draw the object graph before running each cell.
sections.challenge: Implement a safe snapshot helper for a nested configuration and explain which copy policy its API promises.

sections.title: Grammar, expressions, and control flow
sections.why: Understand evaluation order, truthiness, short-circuiting, unpacking, comprehensions, and structural pattern matching.
pitfalls: dense comprehensions that obscure control flow
sections.lab: Instrument expressions to observe evaluation order, then rewrite imperative transforms as readable comprehensions and match statements.
sections.challenge: Design a parser for tagged command dictionaries using match/case with explicit rejection of malformed input.

sections.title: Functions, signatures, closures, and decorators
sections.why: Master Python call semantics and design signatures that are hard to misuse.
pitfalls: decorators that lose signatures or metadata
sections.lab: Inspect signatures, reproduce default and closure traps, and build a wraps-preserving timing decorator.
sections.challenge: Create a retry decorator with a precise API, exception policy, backoff hook, and testable clock.

sections.title: Classes, descriptors, and attribute lookup
sections.why: Explain methods, properties, slots, and framework fields through Python's descriptor protocol and lookup rules.
sections.lab: Implement a validating descriptor and trace __getattribute__, descriptor, instance, and __getattr__ resolution.
sections.challenge: Build a small typed field system without a metaclass and document its inheritance behavior.

sections.title: Iteration, generators, and lazy pipelines
sections.why: Use the iterator protocol to control memory, latency, and resource lifetime.
sections.lab: Create a streaming parser and compare eager and lazy peak memory with tracemalloc.
sections.challenge: Implement a close-safe batched pipeline that propagates errors without leaking its input resource.

sections.title: Exceptions, context managers, and resource safety
sections.why: Design failure paths and deterministic cleanup as part of an API contract.
sections.lab: Build nested context managers and inject failures at every acquisition and release boundary.
sections.challenge: Use ExitStack to atomically acquire a dynamic set of resources with rollback on partial failure.

sections.title: Lists, tuples, deques, and sequence costs
sections.why: Choose sequence representations from operation costs, locality, mutability, and API semantics.
pitfalls: using tuple to imply deep immutability
sections.lab: Benchmark append, insert, pop, slicing, iteration, and random access across list, tuple, and deque.
sections.challenge: Select and justify a bounded event-buffer representation under mixed append, eviction, and scan workloads.

sections.title: Dictionaries, hashing, and cache behavior
sections.why: Understand the hash-table invariants behind Python's most important data structure.
pitfalls: assuming average O(1) is a latency guarantee
sections.lab: Create custom keys, measure collision impact, inspect memory growth, and compare lookup alternatives.
sections.challenge: Implement a bounded LRU-style cache with explicit eviction, metrics, and a safe key contract.

sections.title: Sets, membership, and relational operations
sections.why: Use set algebra to make uniqueness and membership algorithms both clearer and faster.
sections.lab: Replace nested scans with set operations and measure speed, memory, and ordering consequences.
sections.challenge: Design exact and approximate deduplication strategies for a stream with a strict memory budget.

sections.title: Strings, bytes, buffers, and Unicode
sections.why: Separate text from binary protocols and avoid unnecessary encoding and copying.
pitfalls: indexing user-visible characters by code point
sections.lab: Decode incrementally, handle malformed input explicitly, and compare bytes slicing with memoryview.
sections.challenge: Parse a length-prefixed binary frame with bounds checks and zero-copy payload access.

sections.title: Records: dataclasses, named tuples, enums, and models
sections.why: Represent domain state with explicit invariants, equality, ordering, and serialization boundaries.
pitfalls: putting validation only at serialization edges
sections.lab: Model the same domain object several ways and compare memory, mutation, equality, and ergonomics.
sections.challenge: Design an immutable money value object with currency invariants and exact arithmetic.

sections.title: Heaps, bisect, counters, and specialized collections
sections.why: Reach for standard specialized structures before building slower or less reliable custom containers.
pitfalls: mutating sorted data without restoring order
sections.lab: Implement top-k, scheduling, range lookup, and frequency tasks with the standard library.
sections.challenge: Build a stable priority scheduler supporting cancellation and stale-entry compaction.

sections.title: Modules, imports, packages, and dependency boundaries
sections.why: Understand import execution and structure packages that avoid cycles and hidden startup work.
sections.lab: Trace import order, reproduce a cycle, and refactor it around stable interfaces.
sections.challenge: Partition a small application into domain, service, and adapter packages with one-way dependencies.

sections.title: Typing, protocols, generics, and variance
sections.why: Use gradual typing to communicate contracts without confusing static promises with runtime validation.
sections.lab: Type a pluggable repository and decorator, then run a type checker and inspect runtime behavior.
sections.challenge: Design a generic batch API whose input and output variance remains safe for subclasses.

sections.title: Pythonic API design and compatibility
sections.why: Create small, composable interfaces with clear ownership, error, mutation, and evolution contracts.
sections.lab: Critique and redesign several ambiguous APIs; write contracts and compatibility tests first.
sections.challenge: Evolve a synchronous storage API to support batching without breaking existing callers.

sections.title: Testing, debugging, and observability
sections.why: Make behavior and performance regressions reproducible before optimizing or scaling.
sections.lab: Write table, property-style, and fault-injection tests; diagnose a failure from structured logs.
sections.challenge: Build deterministic tests for retries, timeouts, and idempotency using injected clocks and fakes.

sections.title: Serialization, validation, and trust boundaries
sections.why: Treat external data as hostile and keep wire formats separate from domain models.
sections.lab: Validate malformed payloads, add explicit limits, and evolve a versioned message format.
sections.challenge: Design a backward-compatible request envelope with size, type, and authorization boundaries.

sections.title: Packaging, environments, and reproducible delivery
sections.why: Turn code into an installable, isolated, testable artifact with explicit runtime dependencies.
pitfalls: running from the source tree only
sections.lab: Package a small library and CLI, install it into a clean environment, and inspect its metadata.
sections.challenge: Define a release check that verifies wheel contents, imports, CLI behavior, and dependency constraints.

sections.title: The GIL, threads, locks, and memory visibility
sections.why: Choose threads for the right workloads and protect invariants rather than relying on accidental atomicity.
pitfalls: assuming built-in operations make compound logic safe
sections.lab: Force a race, repair it with several synchronization primitives, and measure contention.
sections.challenge: Implement a bounded thread-safe work queue with shutdown, backpressure, and no lost wakeups.

sections.title: Executors, futures, queues, and backpressure
sections.why: Coordinate concurrent work while bounding admission, failures, and shutdown.
sections.lab: Build a bounded executor wrapper and observe queue latency separately from execution latency.
sections.challenge: Design a fan-out pipeline with bounded in-flight work, partial failure policy, and clean cancellation.

sections.title: Processes, IPC, and shared memory
sections.why: Use multiple cores for CPU-bound work while accounting for serialization and process lifecycle costs.
sections.lab: Compare sequential, threaded, and process execution while varying task size and transfer cost.
sections.challenge: Parallelize a CPU-heavy transform with chunking, bounded memory, and deterministic error propagation.

sections.title: Asyncio tasks, event loops, and cancellation
sections.why: Write cooperative asynchronous code that remains responsive and cancellation-safe.
sections.lab: Instrument task interleaving, inject cancellation at each await, and verify resource cleanup.
sections.challenge: Implement structured concurrent fetching with a global timeout, per-host limit, and complete cleanup.

sections.title: Async synchronization, queues, and streaming
sections.why: Apply flow control to asynchronous producers and consumers without thread-style blocking.
sections.lab: Build and monitor a bounded async pipeline with slow consumers and cancellation.
sections.challenge: Create a multi-stage stream processor that drains or aborts predictably on failure.

sections.title: Sockets, TCP streams, and message framing
sections.why: Understand that TCP is a byte stream and build protocols that survive partial reads and writes.
sections.lab: Build a framed echo protocol and deliberately fragment and coalesce writes.
sections.challenge: Implement a bounded binary request protocol with deadlines and malformed-frame recovery.

sections.title: HTTP clients, servers, pooling, and timeouts
sections.why: Design network APIs around connection reuse, deadline budgets, status semantics, and bounded bodies.
sections.lab: Exercise a local HTTP service with delayed headers, slow bodies, disconnects, and pooled connections.
sections.challenge: Build a resilient client wrapper with deadline propagation, safe retries, limits, and observability.

sections.title: Async network services and overload control
sections.why: Keep high-concurrency services stable with admission control, backpressure, and structured shutdown.
sections.lab: Load a local async server, observe queueing, and add limits at connection and request boundaries.
sections.challenge: Implement a service that sheds overload, drains accepted work, and reports saturation metrics.

sections.title: TLS, authentication, and network trust
sections.why: Make confidentiality, peer identity, authorization, and secret handling explicit.
sections.lab: Create secure client/server SSL contexts and verify failures for the wrong hostname or trust root.
sections.challenge: Threat-model and harden a small authenticated network API, including replay and resource-exhaustion defenses.

sections.title: Measurement: timeit, profiling, tracing, and benchmarks
sections.why: Optimize from evidence while separating CPU, wall time, allocation, I/O, and queueing effects.
sections.lab: Build a repeatable benchmark, profile it, form a hypothesis, change one factor, and quantify uncertainty.
sections.challenge: Write a regression benchmark with representative data and a defensible threshold.

sections.title: Algorithmic optimization and data layout
sections.why: Win orders of magnitude through complexity, allocation, locality, and representation before low-level tweaks.
sections.lab: Optimize a slow workload in stages and attribute each gain to algorithm, representation, or constant factor.
sections.challenge: Meet a latency and memory budget for a lookup-and-aggregation workload and defend the chosen structures.

sections.title: High-performance Python capstone
sections.why: Combine API clarity, correctness, concurrency, networking, profiling, and optimization under production constraints.
pitfalls: optimizing one stage while queueing dominates
sections.lab: Build a framed or HTTP service, establish correctness tests and a baseline, then optimize against explicit latency, throughput, CPU, and memory goals.
sections.challenge: Produce a benchmark report explaining bottlenecks, changes, rejected alternatives, failure behavior, and remaining limits.
